info.name: Job Apply API
info.description:
Complete API reference for the Job Application Agent (https://apply.cdlav.us/).

**Auth model:** stateless HMAC-signed `HttpOnly` session cookie (`session`, 30-day TTL). Login/register set the cookie automatically. All protected requests include it. Admins also accepted via `Authorization: Bearer {BOT_API_KEY}` header.

**Run lifecycle (application & prep):**
1. POST to start → returns `run_id` / `prep_id`
2. GET `…/stream` → SSE; events: `progress` (log line), `done` (files + folder_url + optional replacements_warning), `error`
3. `done` event contains `files` with filenames
4. GET `…/files/{name}` → download DOCX

**Rate limits (per IP, sliding window):**
- Login: 10/min
- Register: 5/hr
- Resend-verification: 3/hr
- Change password: 5/hr
- Change email: 5/hr
- Resume upload: 10/hr
- Forgot password: 3/hr
- Reset password: 5/hr

**Security headers on every response:** HSTS (2-year), X-Frame-Options: DENY, X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, CSP, Referrer-Policy.

**Base URL:** `{{base_url}}` defaults to `https://apply.cdlav.us`. Override with `http://localhost:8000` for local dev.

variable.description: Google Drive folder ID from GET /api/gdrive/runs

request.description: Liveness check. Unauthenticated callers get `{"status": "ok"|"degraded"}` only. Authenticated users also receive storage, email, gdrive, model, anthropic, and fly machine details.

item.name: Auth
item.description: Session-based auth using an HMAC-signed `HttpOnly` cookie (30-day TTL). Google OAuth also supported. Changing email or password invalidates existing sessions via a password-version fingerprint (`pwv`) embedded in the token.

request.description: Create account. `multipart/form-data` so resume DOCX can be included. Sets session cookie on success. Sends verification email via Resend. Rate-limited: 5/hr per IP.
Authenticate with email and password. Sets session cookie. Constant-time comparison prevents user-enumeration timing attacks. Rate-limited: 10/min per IP.
Clears session cookie and fly-force-instance-id cookie.
Current user info: `user_id`, `email`, `display_name`, `role`, `email_verified`, `has_resume`, `has_profile`, `model` (active Claude model).

Redirects to Google consent screen. `returnTo` is the path to redirect after auth (clamped to relative paths; admins always go to /admin.html). A nonce is set as an HttpOnly cookie for CSRF protection.

Google OAuth callback. Handled by the browser redirect — do not call directly. Verifies CSRF nonce, exchanges code for tokens, verifies id_token via google-auth, finds or creates user, sets session cookie.
Public endpoint. Consumes a one-time SHA-256-keyed verification token (72h TTL), marks user verified, redirects to /login.html?verified=1. Invalid/expired tokens redirect with an error message.
Resend verification email for the current user. Returns `{"ok": true, "already_verified": true}` if already verified. Rate-limited: 3/hr per IP.
Unauthenticated. Sends a password-reset link to the given email address. Always returns `{"ok": true}` (200) to prevent user enumeration. Rate-limited: 3/hr per IP. The reset link expires in 1 hour.
Unauthenticated. Verifies a one-time reset token (from the forgot-password email) and sets a new password. Token TTL: 1 hour. Rate-limited: 5/hr per IP. Returns `{"ok": true}` on success.

item.name: Profile
item.description: Manage display name, profile guide (Markdown), master resume DOCX, password, and email. Password and email changes are rate-limited and invalidate existing sessions.

request.description: Returns: `display_name`, `email`, `role`, `email_verified`, `profile_text`, `has_resume`, `resume_filename`.
Update display name and/or profile guide text. Both optional. Max display_name: 100 chars; max profile_text: 50,000 chars.
Replace master resume. Must be a .docx file ≥ 1 KB, ≤ 10 MB. Rate-limited: 10/hr per IP.
Change password. Requires current password. Invalidates all existing sessions (password-version fingerprint changes). Sends confirmation email. Rate-limited: 5/hr per IP.

Change email address. Requires current password. Marks account as unverified and sends verification to the new address. Deletes current session cookie — user must log in again. Rate-limited: 5/hr per IP.

item.name: Calendar
item.description:
Create and manage calendar events with optional email/Slack reminders. Events can be linked to application tracker records. Reminders fire via the background scheduler (60s polling).

**Event types:** `interview`, `phone_screen`, `deadline`, `follow_up`, `offer`, `other`, `custom`

**Reminder channels:** `email`, `slack`

**Limits:** 1,000 events/user, 10 reminders/event, offset_minutes 0–525,600 (1 year).

request.description: List all events for the current user. Optional ISO datetime range filters: `from` and `to`. Returns `{events: [...]}` sorted by datetime ascending.
Return up to 20 events in the next 7 days. Used by the Slack App Home tab.

Create a calendar event. `reminders` is an array of `{offset_minutes, channels}` — the scheduler fires each reminder at `event_datetime - offset_minutes`. `app_id` must be a valid application ID owned by the user if provided.

body.raw:
{
  "title": "HM Interview — Salesforce",
  "event_type": "interview",
  "datetime": "2026-06-15T14:00:00Z",
  "timezone": "America/New_York",
  "duration_minutes": 60,
  "notes": "Ask about the agentic roadmap.",
  "app_id": "optional-application-uuid",
  "run_ids": [],
  "reminders": [
    {"offset_minutes": 1440, "channels": ["email", "slack"]},
    {"offset_minutes": 60,   "channels": ["slack"]}
  ]
}

request.description: Get a single event by UUID. Returns 404 if not found or not owned by the current user.

Update any event field. All fields optional. If `datetime` changes and `reminders` is not re-specified, existing reminder fire times are automatically recalculated. If `reminders` is provided, old reminders are deleted and replaced.
Delete an event and all its associated reminders. Returns `{"ok": true}`.

item.name: Application Tracker
item.description:
Full CRUD for job applications. All data is scoped to the authenticated user. Index fields returned by the list endpoint; full records include `comments`, `audit_log`, and `linked_runs`.

**Valid statuses:** Not Applying, Researching, Applied, Phone Screen, Interviewing, On Hold, Offer, Rejected

**Valid priorities:** Low, Medium, High

**Auto-behaviours on PUT:**
- `status → Applied`: sets `date_applied` to today (YYYY-MM-DD) if not already set
- `url` changed: re-runs background JD extraction and saves `job_description.md` to Drive

request.description: List applications (paginated). Optional filters: `status`, `priority`, `page`, `per_page` (0 = all). Returns `{items, total, page, per_page, pages}`.
Create a new application. `url` must start with http:// or https:// if provided. `company_logo_url` is no longer used — logos are rendered dynamically from `domain` via Logo.dev.
Full application record including `comments`, `audit_log`, and `linked_runs` arrays.

Partial update — send only changed fields. Diffs are recorded in the application `audit_log`.

**Side effects:**
- Setting `status: Applied` auto-sets `date_applied` to today if not already present
- Changing `url` triggers background JD extraction: creates/updates the Drive folder and saves `job_description.md`
Delete application. A deletion tombstone is saved before removal for audit purposes. Returns 204.
Returns the application-level `audit_log` array (create, update, delete, comment, run-link events).
Add a comment. Returns the created comment object.
Edit an existing comment.
Delete a comment. Returns 204.
Link a Drive run folder to an application. Auto-called when a run is started with `app_id`.
Unlink a Drive run from an application. Returns 204.

Run (or re-run) Claude-based resume↔JD match scoring. Synchronous — waits for the result. Requires a job description resolvable from the app's linked Drive folder or posting URL, plus an uploaded master resume and profile guide.

Returns `{score, category, dimensions, rationale}`:
- `score`: 0–100
- `category`: `Strong Match` (≥80) | `Good Match` (≥60) | `Stretch` (≥40) | `Long Shot`
- `dimensions`: `{skills, role_type, seniority, differentiators}` (each 0–100)
- `rationale`: 1–2 sentence summary of strongest alignment and biggest gap

Scoring result is persisted to the application record.

Extract job description text from the application's posting URL via Claude. Requires `url` to be set on the application. Returns `{job_posting: "..."}`. Returns 400 if no URL, 422 if extraction fails.

Create a Google Drive folder for this application and attempt to capture `job_description.md` from the posting URL — all in the background. Returns 202 immediately with `{status: "started"}`. The folder is linked to the application record once created. JD extraction is best-effort: LinkedIn and other login-walled URLs will create the folder but not capture a JD.

Logo.dev company search. Returns up to 5 results: `{name, domain, description}`. Logos are constructed client-side from `domain` using `https://img.logo.dev/{domain}?token={pk_}&size={n}&format=png&retina=true`. Used by the tracker and Slack `/track-add` command.

item.name: Application Runs
item.description:
Start and monitor a resume + ATS resume + cover letter generation run.

**Lifecycle:** POST → SSE stream → `done` event contains file names → download files.

Runs are serialised per user (one active at a time per user). Up to 5 in-flight + queued entries per user. Admin accounts cannot create runs.

**`done` event shape:**
```json
{
  "type": "done",
  "run_id": "…",
  "framing_angle": "…",
  "folder_url": "https://drive.google.com/…",
  "replacements_warning": null,
  "files": {
    "resume": "Resume_…docx",
    "ats": "Resume_…_ATS.docx",
    "cover_letter": "CoverLetter_….docx"
  }
}
```
`replacements_warning` is non-null if fewer than 70% of XML edits succeeded — some resume sections may need manual review.

request.description:
Start a resume generation run. Returns {run_id, machine_id}. Set fly-force-instance-id cookie to machine_id before opening the SSE stream to ensure the stream hits the same Fly.io machine.

**JD source (mutually exclusive):**
- `job_posting`: paste the full JD text directly
- `jd_folder_id`: Google Drive folder ID — server loads `job_description.md` from the folder server-side (preferred when re-running from tracker)

If `app_id` is set and `job_posting` was pasted (not loaded via `jd_folder_id`), the server saves `job_description.md` to the run's Drive output folder and registers a `job_description` linked run on the application.

body.raw:
{
  "job_posting": "Paste full JD here…",
  "company": "Acme Corp",
  "role": "Solutions Engineer",
  "contact": "Jane Smith",
  "app_id": "optional-application-uuid",
  "jd_folder_id": null,
  "model": null
}

request.description: SSE stream. Events: `progress` (log line), `done` (files + folder_url + replacements_warning), `error`. Keep-alives every 30s.
Poll run status: `queued | running | done | error`. Returns `{run_id, status, error}`.

Download output DOCX. Filename from `files` in the `done` SSE event. Returns 403 if run belongs to another user (admins can download any run's files). Path-traversal protected via resolve/relative_to check.

item.name: Interview Prep Runs
item.description:
Same lifecycle as Application Runs. `done` event returns `files.prep` (single DOCX). Output is a two-column 9-section reference card: Know Your Interviewer, Role Fit Map, Gap Bridge, Development Framework, Anchor Stories, Likely Questions, Questions to Ask, Differentiating Edge, Closing Line.

**Valid `round_type` values:** `Phone Screen`, `Hiring Manager`, `Peer`, `Technical`, `Executive`, `Panel`

request.description: Start an interview prep run. Returns {prep_id, machine_id}. Set fly-force-instance-id cookie to machine_id before opening the SSE stream.

body.raw:
{
  "job_posting": "Paste full JD here…",
  "company": "Acme Corp",
  "role": "Solutions Engineer",
  "round_type": "Hiring Manager",
  "interviewer": "Jane Smith — Engineering Manager",
  "focus": "Lean into the agentic AI angle",
  "app_id": "optional-application-uuid",
  "model": null
}

request.description: SSE stream for prep run. Same event format as application runs.
Poll prep status: `queued | running | done | error`.
Download prep DOCX. Returns 403 if prep belongs to another user.

item.name: Application Questions
item.description: Answer freeform application questions using the candidate's resume, JD, and profile. Supports tone control, character limits, and a two-phase clarification flow where the agent can ask follow-up questions before generating.

request.description: Start an application question run. Returns {aq_id, machine_id}. The agent may emit a `clarification` SSE event if it needs more context before answering — submit answers via POST /api/aq/{aq_id}/clarify to unblock.

body.raw:
{
  "question": "Describe a time you led a cross-functional team to deliver a complex integration.",
  "job_posting": "Paste full JD here\u2026",
  "company": "Acme Corp",
  "role": "Solutions Engineer",
  "tone": "professional",
  "char_limit": 500,
  "app_id": "optional-application-uuid",
  "model": null
}

request.description: Submit clarification answers to unblock a paused AQ run. Only valid while the run is in `running` status and awaiting clarification. The worker resumes and generates the final answer.
SSE stream for AQ run. Event types: `progress` (status updates), `clarification` (agent needs follow-up answers — submit via /clarify), `done` (includes answer, char_count, follow_ups), `error`.
Poll AQ status: `queued | running | done | error`.
Generate a post-interview thank-you email. Returns `{ty_id, machine_id}`. Output is an editable email body + subject line, saved as DOCX and uploaded to Drive.

body.raw:
{
  "job_posting": "...",
  "company": "Acme Corp",
  "role": "Solutions Engineer",
  "round_type": "Hiring Manager",
  "interviewer": "Sarah Chen",
  "topics": "MCP integration roadmap, scaling connectors",
  "tone": "professional",
  "app_id": "{{app_id}}"
}

request.description: SSE stream for a thank-you email run. Events: `progress`, `done` (includes email_text, subject, files, folder_url), `error`.
Poll thank-you email run status: `queued | running | done | error`.
Download thank-you email DOCX. Returns 403 if run belongs to another user, 404 if run is not complete.

Optimize an existing run's resume and/or cover letter in-place based on a user instruction. `folder_id` must be a Drive folder that belongs to the calling user (verified via application records). Returns `{optimize_id, machine_id}`. Use machine_id to pin subsequent SSE requests. Rate-limited to one active optimize per user.

SSE stream for an optimize run. Event format identical to `/api/run/{id}/stream`. The `done` event includes `change_summary` (list of changes made) and `replacements_warning` (if < 70% of XML edits succeeded). Set `fly-force-instance-id` cookie to `machine_id` from the POST response before opening this stream.
Poll optimize status: `queued | running | done | error`. Returns `files` array and `folder_url` when done.
Download an optimized DOCX. Returns 403 if the optimize run belongs to another user. Filename from `files` in the `done` SSE event.

item.name: Google Drive
item.description:
Proxy endpoints for listing run folders and fetching/saving job descriptions. Drive is configured server-side via GDRIVE_TOKEN_JSON secret (refreshed token persisted to Tigris so it survives container restarts).

Folder ownership is verified against the user's Tigris application records (fast, no Drive API call, no page-size cap).

request.description: List all run folders for the current user from Drive. Checks both the user's personal subfolder and the legacy flat root. Each entry: `{id, name, web_view_link, source: 'user'|'legacy'}`.

Fetch `job_description.md` (or `job_posting.txt` fallback) from a Drive folder. Returns `{job_posting: "..."}`. Returns 404 if no file found. Returns 403 if the folder ID is not linked to any of the user's applications in Tigris.
Upsert job_description.md in a Drive folder. Deletes any existing copy first. Ownership verified.
List structured agent run records for the authenticated user from Tigris S3. Returns {runs: [{id, type, status, company, role, started_at, finished_at, gdrive_folder_url, ...}]}. Sorted newest first.
List all local run folders for the authenticated user, sorted by modification time. Returns {runs: [{folder, has_job_posting}]}.
Fetch the saved job posting text from a local run folder (job_posting.txt). Used by the prep form dropdown.

item.name: Model Config
item.description: Get and set the active Claude model. GET is available to all authenticated users; PUT and list are admin-only.

request.description: Returns `{model, default}` — the currently active model and the compile-time default.
Set the active model (admin only). Persisted to Tigris; takes effect immediately for all new runs.
List all allowed model IDs and the currently active one (admin only).

Returns the current user's audit event log, newest first (up to 500 events). Each event: `{id, action, actor, timestamp, ip, details}`. Events are stored as individual S3 objects — no cross-machine race conditions.

item.name: Admin — Users
item.description:
All endpoints require `role: admin`. Accept the session cookie or `Authorization: Bearer {BOT_API_KEY}`.

Role and active-status changes are immediately reflected because the per-request user cache is invalidated on write.

request.description: List all users with `app_count`, `last_login`, `email_verified`, `active`, `role` status.
Edit `display_name`, `email` (safe rename across all S3 indexes), `role`, `email_verified`, `active`. Changes are reflected immediately — user cache is invalidated after write.
Resend verification email to a specific user as admin.
Set only the role of a user (`user` or `admin`). Simpler alternative to `PUT /api/admin/users/{id}` — accepted by the Slack bot for role management. Invalidates the user record cache.
Return all applications belonging to a specific user. Equivalent to filtering `GET /api/admin/applications` by user_id but scoped to a single user.
All applications across all users. Each item includes `_user_id` and `_user_email`.
Full application record for any user.
Admin update any application.
Admin delete any application. Returns 204.
Admin add comment to any application.

All agent run records across all users from the persistent AgentRun store. Each record includes id, type (resume/interview_prep/aq/optimize/thank_you/scoring), status, started_at, finished_at, company, role, user_id, user_email, app_id, initiated_by, gdrive_folder_id, gdrive_folder_url, output_files, score, score_category, score_reasoning, round_type, optimize_instruction, and error. In-memory status is overlaid for currently active runs.

Unified paginated audit log across all users and all application records. Each event is an individual S3 object (no cross-machine write races). Filters: `action`, `actor`, `user_id`, `source` (user|application), `event_id`, `from_ts`, `to_ts` (ISO), `sort_order` (asc|desc).
Full filtered audit result with no pagination. Same filters as GET /api/admin/audit. Logs `admin_csv_export` event. Used for CSV/Excel exports from the admin dashboard.
Returns sorted list of all known audit action type strings for filter dropdowns.
Log a custom admin activity event (e.g. CSV export triggered from the browser). Writes `admin_csv_export` to the audit log.

item.name: Admin — Webhooks
item.description:
Create and manage outbound webhooks for event streaming. Webhooks fire on every `user_audit.log()` call that matches subscribed events and active filters.

**Payload formats:** `generic` | `slack` | `ms_teams` | `grafana_loki`

**Filters (all AND, blank = skip):**
- `filter_actors`: comma-separated emails/user_ids
- `filter_source`: `user` | `application`
- `filter_categories`: array of `auth|profile|applications|runs|admin`
- `filter_app_id`: specific application UUID

**Security:** `secret` is stored encrypted at rest with AES-256-GCM. If set, each delivery includes `X-Hub-Signature-256: sha256={hmac}`. Rotating `SESSION_SECRET` invalidates stored secrets — re-save each webhook to re-encrypt. SSRF guard is applied at both creation time and delivery time.

request.description: List all webhooks (summary from index — secrets not included).
Create a webhook. `url` is validated against SSRF (DNS resolution + private-net check). `secret` is encrypted with AES-256-GCM before storage.
Full webhook record including `recent_deliveries` (last 25) and `delivery_stats`. Secret is redacted in the response (first/last 4 chars shown).
Update any webhook field. All fields optional. Re-saving with a `secret` re-encrypts it under the current SESSION_SECRET.
Delete webhook. Returns 204.

Send a synchronous test delivery with `action: webhook_test`. Returns delivery result: `{id, timestamp, event_action, status_code, success, error, duration_ms}`. SSRF guard is applied before the request is sent.

Last 25 deliveries for a webhook, sorted descending by timestamp. Each entry includes `status_code`, `success`, `error`, `duration_ms`. Blocked deliveries (SSRF) appear with `error: Delivery blocked: URL resolved to a private/internal address`.

item.name: Knowledge Base
item.description: Public (authenticated) endpoints for browsing KB articles and categories. The KB is stored as a single JSON blob in Tigris (`kb/data.json`). Seed data is auto-applied when no KB has been saved yet.

request.description: List all KB articles and categories. Returns `{ categories: [...], articles: [...] }`. Articles with `adminOnly: true` are included in the payload but the frontend filters them for non-admin users.
Fetch a single KB article by its string ID (e.g. `getting-started`). Returns 404 if not found.
List KB categories only (no article bodies). Useful for rendering the sidebar nav without loading full article content.

item.name: Admin — Knowledge Base
item.description:
Admin-only endpoints for managing KB articles and categories. All writes are persisted to Tigris (`kb/data.json`) and reflected immediately on the next `GET /api/kb/articles` call.

**Article fields:** `id` (auto-generated slug if omitted), `category` (must match a category `id`), `title`, `snippet` (plain-text teaser), `read_time`, `body` (HTML, supports Quill output), `admin_only`.

**Category fields:** `id` (kebab-case), `label`, `icon` (emoji), `desc`, `admin_only`.

request.description: Create a new KB article. `id` is optional — omit to auto-generate a short UUID. Returns 400 if the `id` already exists.

body.raw:
{ "id": "my-article", "category": "getting-started", "title": "How to run the agent", "snippet": "Step-by-step overview of the apply workflow.", "read_time": "3 min", "body": "

Body HTML here.

", "admin_only": false }

request.description: Update an existing KB article. All fields are optional — only provided fields are changed. Returns 404 if the article doesn't exist.
Delete a KB article. Returns 204 on success, 404 if not found.
Create a new KB category. `id` is normalized to kebab-case. Returns 400 if the ID already exists.

body.raw:
{
  "id": "new-category",
  "label": "New Category",
  "icon": "\ud83d\udccc",
  "desc": "Short description shown in the sidebar.",
  "admin_only": false
}

request.description: Update a KB category's label, icon, description, or adminOnly flag. All fields optional. Returns 404 if not found.
Delete a KB category. Returns 204 on success, 404 if not found. Note: does not cascade-delete articles in that category.
Replace the entire KB (categories + articles) with the provided JSON payload. Used by the admin UI on first load to hydrate Tigris from the frontend constant. Timestamps are set automatically.

Server-side extraction: runs a Node.js script to parse the `const KB = {...}` object out of `frontend/kb.html` and seeds Tigris. Used to sync the hardcoded frontend KB into persistent storage without manually copying JSON. Requires Node.js to be present on the server.

query.description: Signed HMAC notification token from email link

request.description: Unauthenticated. Verifies a signed one-time notification token and executes the encoded action. Used in outbound nudge/follow-up email links. Actions: `status` (update application status), `snooze` (suppress notifications for N days). For `status=Applied` without a `date_applied` in the token, redirects to `/confirm-applied.html?token=…` so the user can pick the date. All other actions redirect to `#tracker`. Token TTL is set at issue time (default 7 days).
